_comment: Scenario probes for P1 (single practitioner, dynamic location) — expanded to full crossover-checklist depth (see .claude/rules/node3-slim-crossover-checklist.md Section 2), matching P4's battery shape. Covers CONFIRMATION, slot-offer counts, wrong-intent guard, year-anchor, WHO IS IT FOR / IDENTITY CORRECTION, all escape routes, message-collection routes, tool-result handling, slot-offer edge cases, and response/resume handling.

scenarios.name: C1 — Confirmation: filler phrase + universal_router confirm_time tool call in the same turn

history.content: Can I book a physio appointment on Friday afternoon?
When would you like to come in?
Friday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-19", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "2:00 PM", "3:00 PM", "4:00 PM", "5:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
The 1pm one please

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if: agent speaks exactly one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set ('One moment.' / 'Just a sec.' / 'Let me check that.') and calls universal_router intent='confirm_time' in the SAME response turn, per the node's CONFIRMATION rule and the shared TOOL-CALL FILLER rule. FAIL if: agent adds anything beyond the filler phrase (e.g. repeating the appointment date/time/practitioner, saying 'great', 'I'll book you in', 'you're booked', or asking 'anything else?'), calls smart_voice_agent instead of universal_router, is truly silent with no filler phrase at all, or splits the filler phrase and the tool call across two separate turns.

scenarios.name: S1 — Slot offer count: exactly 2 times spoken (first and last only)

history.content: I'd like something on Monday
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["8:00 AM", "9:00 AM", "10:00 AM", "11:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks exactly two times: 8:00 AM and 11:00 AM (first and last), using the form 'I've got 8:00 AM or 11:00 AM — does either of those work for you?' FAIL if agent lists 3 or more times (e.g. '8:00 AM, 9:00 AM, 10:00 AM, or 11:00 AM'), or lists only one time when 4 are available.

scenarios.name: N1 — Find-next-available: asap_slots intent on 'next available' request

history.content: What's the next available appointment?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent says 'Checking that now, one moment.' and calls smart_voice_agent with intent='asap_slots' and a start_date derived from {{current_time_local}}. FAIL if agent calls with intent='availability' (missing date), intent='find_next_available', or offers hardcoded dates without calling the tool.

scenarios.name: Y1 — Year anchor: date labelled using current_time_local year, not training-data year

history.content: Do you have anything next Monday?
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": "9:00 AM"}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
Yes, 9am works

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the universal_router confirm_time payload has appointment_date='2026-06-22', matching the cached slot's date, per TIMEFRAME DERIVATION ('the year is authoritative, never infer it from training data') and CONFIRMATION (payload uses the date field of the chosen slot). FAIL if the year in the payload is 2025 or any year other than 2026, indicating the node ignored {{current_time_local}} and inferred the year from training data. Per the corrected CONFIRMATION rule, the spoken output is filler-only ('One moment.' / 'Just a sec.' / 'Let me check that.') and will never contain the year — do not fail on absence of '2026' in speech.

scenarios.name: W1 — Wrong intent: confirm via universal_router not smart_voice_agent

history.content: Anything on Monday?
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_abc", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["8:00 AM", "9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 8:00 AM or 9:00 AM — does either of those work for you?
Yes, 8am please

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if agent speaks the filler phrase and calls universal_router intent='confirm_time' for the confirmation, per the CONFIRMATION rule and the Booking completion hard rule ('the ONLY valid action is the CONFIRMATION tool call ... universal_router intent="confirm_time"'). FAIL if agent calls smart_voice_agent with any intent to confirm the time — smart_voice_agent only fetches availability and never books.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-1 — BOOKING_FOR GATE: caller names a specific third party directly, no confirm question

history.content: Hi, I need to book my son Jake in for a physio session

scenarios.expected_behavior: Assume {{booking_for}}='other'. PASS if the agent does not ask 'Is this appointment still for the same person, or is it for you this time?' — the RECENT CONTEXT SCAN in the BOOKING_FOR GATE already found the explicit third-party reference 'my son Jake' — and instead asks the timeframe question in third person, e.g. 'When would Jake like to come in?' FAIL if it asks the confirmation question after Jake was already named, or if it addresses Jake in second person.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-2 — BOOKING_FOR GATE: ambiguous, must ask the exact confirmation question

history.content: Yep that one sounds good

scenarios.expected_behavior: Assume {{booking_for}}='other' and no third-party reference has been made yet, and no agent turn has been spoken yet. PASS if the agent's entire turn is exactly 'Is this appointment still for the same person, or is it for you this time?' with no other content. FAIL if it adds any other sentence, skips the question, or asks something differently worded.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-3 — IDENTITY CORRECTION: mid-conversation person swap, no re-confirm question

history.content: It's for my son
When would they like to come in?
Actually wait, it's for my daughter Emma instead

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent updates booking_for_name to Emma and re-asks the timeframe question using her name ('When would Emma like to come in?') without asking 'Is this still for the same [person] as before?' FAIL if it asks a redundant confirmation question, or fails to switch to Emma's name.

scenarios.name: ENTRY-4 — IDENTITY CORRECTION: correction names an animal, SPECIES SWITCH to change_service

history.content: It's for my son
When would they like to come in?
Oh wait sorry, I actually meant my dog, he needs a check up

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set only, then calls universal_router intent='change_service' in the same turn — nothing else spoken. FAIL if the agent continues the booking flow for the dog, asks a clarifying question, or speaks any content beyond the filler phrase.

scenarios.name: ESC-1 — TREATMENT-TYPE QUESTION confirmed by SERVICE_CATEGORIES: answer inline, re-ask, no route

history.content: Quick one — do you guys do physiotherapy there?

scenarios.expected_behavior: SERVICE_CATEGORIES='PHYSIOTHERAPY'. PASS if the agent briefly confirms physiotherapy is offered and re-asks whatever the currently active question was, without calling universal_router. FAIL if it routes to intent='info_pivot' for a treatment type SERVICE_CATEGORIES already confirms, or guesses without confirming.

scenarios.name: ESC-2 — TREATMENT-TYPE QUESTION unconfirmable: must route to info_pivot, not guess

history.content: Do you guys do dry needling there?

scenarios.expected_behavior: SERVICE_CATEGORIES='PHYSIOTHERAPY' does not name dry needling or an obvious synonym. PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set then calls universal_router intent='info_pivot' — it must not guess yes or no. FAIL if the agent confidently confirms or denies dry needling is offered without routing.

scenarios.name: ESC-3 — PRICING QUERY carve-out: plain cost question is NOT info_pivot

history.content: How much does it cost for a session?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent does NOT call universal_router intent='info_pivot' for this plain pricing question — the system prompt's own PRICING QUERY block owns plain cost/fee questions directly per the info_pivot carve-out. FAIL if the agent routes this to info_pivot as if it were a policy question.

scenarios.name: ESC-4 — CONSTRAINT PIVOT RULE: same recurring weekday, requested_weekday preserved

history.content: Do you have any Saturdays available?
Let me check that.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "find_next_available", "session_id": "sess_sat", "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-20", "day_of_week": "Saturday", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "10:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got Saturday the 20th with 9:00 AM or 10:00 AM in the morning — does either of those work?
Do you have anything on an earlier Saturday?

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says 'Checking that now, one moment.' (or equivalent) and calls smart_voice_agent intent='find_next_available' with requested_weekday='Saturday' UNCHANGED per CONSTRAINT PIVOT RULE. FAIL if it calls universal_router intent='change_time' instead (ejecting the caller from the node), or drops requested_weekday.

scenarios.name: ESC-5 — CONSTRAINT CHANGE: explicit day swap uses change_time, filler only

history.content: Wednesday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_wed", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-17", "day_of_week": "Wednesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-17", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "2:00 PM", "4:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 1:00 PM or 4:00 PM on Wednesday the 17th — does either of those work for you?
Actually, make it Thursday instead of Wednesday

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks exactly one filler phrase (e.g. 'One moment.') and calls universal_router intent='change_time' in the same turn, with nothing else spoken. FAIL if it speaks extra content, asks a clarifying question first, or calls smart_voice_agent directly instead of routing through change_time.

scenarios.name: ESC-6 — SERVICE PIVOT: caller abandons the attempt, filler only

history.content: When would you like to come in?
Actually you know what, never mind, forget it

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set only, then calls universal_router intent='change_service' in the same turn. FAIL if it asks why, tries to keep the caller booking, or adds spoken content beyond the filler.

scenarios.name: ESC-8 — SERVICE PIVOT EXCLUSION: slot already offered, bare abandonment routes to AVAILABILITY ABANDON not change_service

history.content: Monday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_mon3", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "11:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 9:00 AM or 11:00 AM on Monday — does either of those work for you?
Actually you know what, never mind, forget it

scenarios.expected_behavior: A time slot has already been offered this call (9:00 AM / 11:00 AM), and the caller declines with pure abandonment language, naming no different service or treatment. PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set then calls universal_router intent='abandon_availability' — per SERVICE PIVOT's EXCLUSION, once a slot has been offered this call, bare 'never mind'/'forget it' routes to AVAILABILITY ABANDON, not SERVICE PIVOT. FAIL if the agent calls intent='change_service' instead (that would incorrectly eject the caller into service reselection after they were already mid-booking with a slot on the table). Contrast with ESC-6, which tests the same phrase BEFORE any slot has been offered — there, change_service is correct.

scenarios.name: ESC-9 — EARLIER DATE SEARCH: requested_weekday preserved even with generic 'anything sooner' phrasing (no day named)

history.content: Do you have any Tuesdays available?
Let me check that.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "find_next_available", "session_id": "sess_tue3", "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-30", "day_of_week": "Tuesday", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "10:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got Tuesday the 30th with 9:00 AM or 10:00 AM in the morning — does either of those work?
Hmm, is there anything sooner than that?

scenarios.expected_behavior: requested_weekday='Tuesday' is active from the earlier 'any Tuesdays' request, and a slot has already been offered. The caller's rephrasing ('anything sooner') does not repeat the word 'Tuesday'. PASS if the agent says 'Let me check that.' (or equivalent) and calls smart_voice_agent intent='find_next_available' with requested_weekday='Tuesday' UNCHANGED. FAIL if it calls intent='asap_slots' instead, silently dropping the weekday constraint, or calls universal_router intent='change_time' and ejects the caller from the node.

scenarios.name: ESC-7 — NEW BOOKING after cancellation: caller wants to book again once cancellation_completed

history.content: Cancel my appointment please
[cancellation handled in another node, cancellation_completed set to true]
[returned to Node 3 with {{cancellation_completed}}='true']
Actually, I'd like to book a new one instead

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set only, then calls universal_router intent='new_booking' in the same turn. FAIL if it starts a fresh timeframe question itself without routing, or uses a different intent.

scenarios.name: MSG-1 — CALLBACK REQUEST: filler + tool same turn, no premature promise

history.content: Actually can someone just call me back about billing?

scenarios.expected_behavior: Assume {{patient_name_raw}} is already populated. PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set and calls universal_router intent='callback_request' with payload containing caller_name and callback_reason='billing' in the SAME turn, adding nothing beyond the filler — critically NOT saying 'I'll let them know' or 'I'll pass that on' before the tool result returns. FAIL if the agent promises the callback was made before calling the tool.

scenarios.name: MSG-2 — RUNNING LATE: name known, spoken phrase + tool call in the same turn

history.content: Hi it's Jordan Lee, I'm running about ten minutes late for my appointment

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says 'I'll let them know you're on your way.' and calls universal_router intent='running_late' with payload caller_name='Jordan Lee' in the SAME turn. FAIL if it splits the phrase and tool call across turns, produces zero spoken output, or asks for the name again when it was already given.

scenarios.name: MSG-3 — LEAVE MESSAGE: terminal routing uses abandon_availability, not wrap_up

history.content: Can I just leave a message instead?
Sure, what would you like the message to say?
Just tell them I'll call back next week
Can I get your name for the message?
It's Sam Patel
One moment.
[universal_router response]: {"message": "Got it, I've passed that message on for you."}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks the tool's response message verbatim ('Got it, I've passed that message on for you.') and then calls universal_router intent='abandon_availability' — NOT intent='wrap_up'. FAIL if it uses wrap_up, or claims the message was passed on before this tool result existed.

scenarios.name: TR-1 — STEP 2 DETAILS_PAST RETURN: silent practitioner store, no announcement

history.content: Can I book with the same physio I saw last time?
One moment.
[universal_router response, intent=details_past]: {"practitioner_preference": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent stores Sarah Jones as confirmed_practitioner silently — it does NOT announce 'your practitioner is Sarah Jones' (single-practitioner clinic — do not announce the match) — and proceeds to STEP 3 (timeframe question) if {{timeframe_raw}} is unset, or continues toward STEP 4 if already set. FAIL if it announces the practitioner match or asks the caller to confirm the practitioner.

scenarios.name: TR-2 — STEP 2 found=false: apology and stop, no auto-retry

history.content: Next Tuesday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": false, "response_type": "availability"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent says something equivalent to 'I'm sorry, there's no availability for that period. Would you like to try a different time?' and stops — no tool call this turn. FAIL if it calls a tool again automatically, or fails to invite a different time.

scenarios.name: TR-3 — STEP 2 response_type=summary: speak message verbatim, ask which day, no self-listing

history.content: Sometime next month please
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "summary", "message": "Sarah has availability on most weekdays next month, mainly mornings.", "session_id": "sess_month"}

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent speaks the message field verbatim and adds 'Which day works for you?' — nothing more, no self-generated date list. FAIL if it invents specific dates itself instead of relying on the message field.

scenarios.name: SLOT-2 — TIME PROXIMITY EXCEPTION 'before 4pm': single nearest slot, not the first/last pair

history.content: Something before 4pm on Friday
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_fri", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-19", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["12:00 PM", "1:30 PM", "3:00 PM", "5:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: offered_slots = [12:00 PM, 1:30 PM, 3:00 PM, 5:00 PM]. PASS ONLY if the agent offers exactly 3:00 PM (nearest at-or-before 4pm) alone: 'I've got 3:00 PM — does that work?' FAIL if it offers 5:00 PM, or offers the first/last pair (12:00 PM / 5:00 PM), ignoring the TIME PROXIMITY EXCEPTION.

scenarios.name: SLOT-3 — TIME PROXIMITY EXCEPTION 'around 2pm': pair from both sides

history.content: Something around 2pm on Monday
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_mon", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "3:00 PM", "4:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: offered_slots = [1:00 PM, 3:00 PM, 4:00 PM]. PASS ONLY if the agent offers exactly 1:00 PM and 3:00 PM (nearest before and after 2pm): 'I've got 1:00 PM or 3:00 PM — does either work?' FAIL if it includes 4:00 PM, or offers only one time when both sides have a slot.

scenarios.name: SLOT-4 — STEP 9 1-time branch: 1 time + next band available, both options offered

history.content: Tuesday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_tue", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-16", "day_of_week": "Tuesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-16", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["11:30 AM"], "afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "2:30 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: confirmed_band=morning has exactly 1 time (11:30 AM); the next band (afternoon) has times. PASS if the agent offers both, e.g. 'I only have 11:30 AM in the morning, or there's also 1:00 PM in the afternoon if that works?' FAIL if it offers only the single morning time without mentioning the afternoon option.

scenarios.name: SLOT-5 — STEP 9 1-time branch: no next band, explicit affirmative required, weak filler rejected

history.content: Friday evening please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_fri2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-19", "slot_groups": {"evening": ["6:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
Friday evening I only have 6:00 PM — would that work for you?
hmm okay

scenarios.expected_behavior: evening is the last band (no next band per morning->afternoon->evening ordering), and the caller's reply is a weak filler, not an explicit affirmative. PASS if the agent does NOT treat 'hmm okay' as a confirmation and instead re-asks 'Would 6:00 PM work?' with no tool call. FAIL if it proceeds to CONFIRMATION off a bare filler.

scenarios.name: SLOT-6 — MULTI-DAY RESPONSE: 2+ bands present across dates, ask one band question

history.content: Thursday or Friday please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "multi_day": true, "session_id": "sess_multi", "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-18", "day_of_week": "Thursday", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}, {"date": "2026-06-19", "day_of_week": "Friday", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["2:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: Thursday has only morning, Friday has only afternoon — union of bands present = {morning, afternoon}, two bands. PASS if the agent asks ONE question naming only those two bands: 'Would you prefer morning or afternoon?' and stops — no date list yet. FAIL if it lists specific dates/times before asking, or names a band not present (e.g. evening).

scenarios.name: SLOT-7 — FIRST APPOINTMENT EXCEPTION: band question skipped, single earliest time offered

history.content: What's the first appointment available on Saturday?
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_sat2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-20", "day_of_week": "Saturday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-20", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM", "11:00 AM"], "afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "3:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller asked for the first/earliest appointment on a named day (FIRST APPOINTMENT EXCEPTION) and the result has 2 bands with times (morning, afternoon). PASS ONLY if the agent does NOT ask 'Would you prefer morning or afternoon?' — it must skip the band question entirely, silently select morning as confirmed_band (it contains the globally-earliest time, 9:00 AM), and offer only that single earliest time: 'The first available Saturday is at 9:00 AM — does that work?' FAIL if the agent asks the band-preference question first, or if it offers a first/last pair instead of the single earliest time.

scenarios.name: RESP-1 — Ambiguous affirmative after a two-time offer: clarify, no tool call

history.content: Wednesday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller confirmed without naming a specific time, and two times were offered. PASS if the agent asks a clarifying question like 'Yes the 1:00 PM or yes the 4:00 PM?' with no tool call. FAIL if it guesses one of the two times and proceeds straight to confirm_time.

scenarios.name: RESP-2 — STEP 7 PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK minimal pair: same period repeats, different period re-searches

history.content: Friday the 19th please
Checking that now, one moment.
[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": false, "response_type": "availability"}
I'm sorry, there's no availability for that period. Would you like to try a different time?
Really nothing at all on Friday the 19th?

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller re-asked about the EXACT SAME date already declared unavailable this call. PASS if the agent responds from memory with no new tool call. FAIL if it calls smart_voice_agent again for the identical already-checked date. (Contrast: a bare weekday like 'what about a Friday?' or a different week would correctly trigger a new search — but that is not this scenario.)

scenarios.name: RESP-3 — EXHAUSTED OPTIONS terminal exit: nothing left, routes to abandon_availability

history.content: Monday morning please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_mon2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-22", "day_of_week": "Monday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-22", "slot_groups": {"morning": ["9:00 AM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
Monday morning I only have 9:00 AM — would that work?
No, that doesn't work
Happy to check another day — what suits you?
No honestly there's nothing else I can do, forget it

scenarios.expected_behavior: The caller has declined the only offered time and then declined to try anything else, with nothing left in the cache. PASS if the agent speaks one filler phrase from the TOOL-CALL FILLER set and calls universal_router intent='abandon_availability'. FAIL if it keeps prompting for another day, or calls a different intent.

Wait, which Wednesday is that?

scenarios.name: RESP-6 — RESUME FROM CONSTRAINT CHANGE: new day, no duplicate change_* call, fetches if not cached

history.content: Wednesday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_wed4", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-17", "day_of_week": "Wednesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-17", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "4:00 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 1:00 PM or 4:00 PM on Wednesday the 17th — does either of those work for you?
Actually, make it Thursday instead
One moment.
[universal_router response, intent=change_time processed]: {}
[returned to Node 3 with {{constraint_changed}}='true', caller's last message was "Actually, make it Thursday instead"]

scenarios.expected_behavior: PASS if the agent does NOT call universal_router with a change_* intent again (routing already handled), updates confirmed_day to Thursday, clears confirmed_band/confirmed_time/offered_slots, and — since Thursday is not yet in cache — says 'Checking that now, one moment.' and calls smart_voice_agent for Thursday. FAIL if it re-calls universal_router intent='change_time', or asks the caller a redundant clarifying question instead of proceeding with the already-stated new day.

scenarios.name: RESP-7 — Relative same-day move: 'anything later in the day?' offers next cached time, no unavailability claim

history.content: Tuesday afternoon please
Checking that now, one moment.

[smart_voice_agent response]: {"found": true, "response_type": "availability", "session_id": "sess_tue2", "first_available": {"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio", "date": "2026-06-16", "day_of_week": "Tuesday", "time": null}, "practitioners": [{"practitioner_name": "Sarah Jones", "practitioner_id": "prac_1", "dates": [{"date": "2026-06-16", "slot_groups": {"afternoon": ["1:00 PM", "3:00 PM", "4:30 PM"]}, "business_id": "biz_1", "business_name": "Northside Physio"}]}]}
I've got 1:00 PM or 4:30 PM on Tuesday the 16th — does either of those work for you?
Is there anything later in the day?

scenarios.expected_behavior: This is a RELATIVE-BAND request (same day, later time), not a PERIOD-SWITCH — the PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK must NOT fire. PASS if the agent checks the cached slot_groups for Tuesday and reports there is nothing later than 4:30 PM (the offered pair was already first/last) — e.g. 'That's the last available on Tuesday, I'm afraid' — then offers another day or a callback, all without a new tool call (data already cached) or a false claim the day is fully unavailable. FAIL if it treats this as a PERIOD-SWITCH and applies PRIOR-UNAVAILABILITY CHECK, or claims no times exist on Tuesday when 1:00 PM/3:00 PM/4:30 PM are cached.
